title: CHAT_USING constant allocates Vec<String> on every request
description: In methods.rs, build_request does: CHAT_USING.iter().map(|s| s.to_string()).collect() to populate JmapRequest::using. This happens on every single JMAP method call. The constant exists to avoid writing the URIs twice, but the realization into Vec<String> is deferred into the hot path. The real problem is that JmapRequest::using is Vec<String> when the call-time value is always a statically-known slice. Rewrite: change JmapRequest::using to Vec<String> but construct it once in a lazy_static or simply inline the two well-known strings as vec![...to_string()] at construction time and document that build_request is only ever called with the standard set. Alternatively, keep the constant and use CHAT_USING.iter().map(|&s| s.to_string()) without the intermediate &str hop. The allocation itself is minor; the real elegance issue is that the pattern implies reuse across different using sets but the code only ever uses one set.
close_reason: Labeled OPINION by SEC7: allocation is noise vs HTTP round-trip cost
